behavior_summary: This script implements the behavior for a 'magic armor' item: a simple U8P_MAGARM::look/examine handler plus an autonomous proximity-driven movement routine that runs while an actor is near.
U8P_MAGARM::look is a short process that displays the single bark text 'magic armor' when the player examines the item.
When an actor enters the item's fast-area, U8P_MAGARM::enterFastArea checks the item's status and (if allowed) starts an active behavior by calling U8P_MAGARM::func00F4.

U8P_MAGARM::func00F4 is the core active routine: it clears an item status bit (~0x04), measures range to a reference (likely the avatar), computes X/Y offsets (directed toward the reference or randomized if a status bit 0x08 is set), clamps offsets, checks Item::legal_move for the target, attempts alternate rotated moves if blocked, and falls back to Item::hurl when movement is not possible. It also uses urandom for jitter and probabilistic hurl events and spawns/schedules a timer process to repeat behavior.
U8P_MAGARM::leaveFastArea performs cleanup by resetting/purging the spawned/timer reference (FREE::resetRef) so the active behavior stops when actors leave the trigger.
notable_control_flow: U8P_MAGARM::enterFastArea reads the item's status (Item::getStatus) and only calls U8P_MAGARM::func00F4 when status bit 0x04 is clear (guard that prevents duplicate starts).

U8P_MAGARM::func00F4 begins by clearing the item's 0x04 bit (Item::andStatus(~0x04)), then branches on proximity (Item::getRange(0x01) compared against a threshold) deciding between directed movement and jitter/random behavior.

Movement attempt uses computed offsets (local9/local10) that are clamped then passed to Item::legal_move. If legal_move fails the code rotates/swaps offsets and retries; if still blocked it invokes Item::hurl as a fallback.

U8P_MAGARM::func00F4 uses urandom to add small random offsets and to occasionally trigger a hurl instead of a legal move, and it spawns/schedules another process (spawn -> FREE::func1C19) to repeat behavior after a delay.
U8P_MAGARM::leaveFastArea calls FREE::resetRef(0x00F0h, this) to cancel the repeating/spawned process (cleanup/stop).
open_questions: Exact semantics and meanings of the per-item status bits (0x04, 0x08) beyond their observed uses (guard vs 'randomize' flag) — names and broader side effects are not present in the data.

Precise numeric thresholds and clamping constants used in U8P_MAGARM::func00F4 (comparisons like range vs 0x0080 and clamp magnitude +/-0x40) — these values are inferred but their tuning effects in gameplay are uncertain.
Identity of the 'reference' used for range/center calculations (Item::getCX/Item::getCY) — likely the avatar but the code doesn't name it explicitly here.
Exact timing/period of the spawned timer (FREE::func1C19) and how it affects perceived movement frequency.
Whether Item::hurl uses additional side effects (damage, collision) when invoked by this item or is purely positional.

main_function.name: U8P_MAGARM::look
main_function.purpose: Present the short examine/description bark for the item (displays 'magic armor').
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine when the player examines or 'looks at' the item (U8P_MAGARM::look/examine event/processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_MAGARM::look
function_map.role: Present examination text; UI-facing brief description ('magic armor').

function_map.name: U8P_MAGARM::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Trigger handler run when an actor enters the item's fast-area; guarded starter for active behavior (calls U8P_MAGARM::func00F4 when allowed).

function_map.name: U8P_MAGARM::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Cleanup handler run when actor leaves fast-area; cancels the active repeating process/handle (calls FREE::resetRef).

function_map.name: U8P_MAGARM::func00F4
function_map.role: Core active behavior routine that drives proximity-based movement/jitter and fallback hurling; uses randomness and spawns timers to repeat.

internal_calls.purpose: Start the item's active proximity behavior when fast-area entry is valid/allowed.

external_calls.purpose: Read per-item status flags to decide whether to start active behavior (test bit 0x04).
Kick off the repeated/active routine (internal but represented externally in the flow).
Reset/cancel the spawned/timer reference so the active behavior stops when actors leave range.
Clear per-item status bit ~0x04 at the start of active behavior (write to item's status flags).
Measure distance to a reference (param 0x01) to branch behavior by proximity.
Read reference center X when computing directed offsets toward/relative to the reference.
Read reference center Y when computing directed offsets toward/relative to the reference.
Generate random values used for jitter in offsets and chance-based hurl events.
Read the item's current X coordinate to compute target X for movement/hurl.
Read the item's current Y coordinate to compute target Y for movement/hurl.
Read the item's Z (height) used in movement/hurl decision paths (vertical checks).
Test whether a computed target X/Y is a legal move before attempting movement; guides alternate attempts or fallback to hurl.
Forcefully move/throw the item if normal movement is blocked or per random-hurl conditions.
Spawn a timer/continuation to schedule repeated invocations of the behavior (creates a repeating effect).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the behavior for a 'magic armor' item: a simple U8P_MAGARM::look/examine handler plus an autonomous proximity-driven movement routine that runs while an actor is near.
